entities.name: APOE
entities.description: Apolipoprotein E, major risk promoting gene in Alzheimer disease related to herpes simplex life cycle

entities.name: CLU
entities.description: Clusterin gene, major risk promoting gene in Alzheimer disease

entities.name: CR1
entities.description: Complement receptor 1 gene, major risk promoting gene in Alzheimer disease

entities.name: PICALM
entities.description: Phosphatidylinositol binding clathrin assembly protein, major AD risk gene related to herpes simplex life cycle

entities.name: APP
entities.description: Amyloid precursor protein gene, involved in processing and signalling disrupted by HSV-1

entities.name: MAPT
entities.description: Tau protein gene, involved in microtubule stability; tau phosphorylation is a core AD pathology

entities.name: ADNP
entities.description: Activity-dependent neuroprotective protein, sequestered by HSV-1

entities.name: Alzheimer disease
entities.description: Neurodegenerative disease characterized by amyloid plaques and neurofibrillary tangles

entities.name: herpes simplex encephalitis
entities.description: HSV-1 infection of the brain, proposed contributor to Alzheimer disease pathology

entities.name: amyloid beta
entities.description: Beta-amyloid peptide that accumulates in plaques in Alzheimer disease

entities.name: tau
entities.description: Microtubule-associated protein that becomes hyperphosphorylated in neurofibrillary tangles

entities.name: apolipoprotein E
entities.description: Protein encoded by APOE, major AD risk factor and HSV-1 related protein

entities.name: clusterin
entities.description: Complement-associated glycoprotein encoded by CLU

entities.name: complement receptor 1
entities.description: Complement receptor encoded by CR1

entities.name: complement membrane attack complex
entities.description: Terminal complement complex found in AD neurones suggesting complement-mediated neuronal lysis

entities.name: amyloid plaques
entities.description: Extracellular deposits of amyloid beta peptide, hallmark of Alzheimer disease

entities.name: neurofibrillary tangles
entities.description: Intraneuronal aggregates of hyperphosphorylated tau protein

entities.name: tau phosphorylation
entities.description: Hyperphosphorylation of tau protein, core pathology of AD increased by HSV-1 infection

entities.name: cortical neuronal loss
entities.description: Loss of cortical neurons observed in HSV-1 infected mice

entities.name: hippocampal neuronal loss
entities.description: Loss of hippocampal neurons observed in HSV-1 infected mice

entities.name: cerebral atrophy
entities.description: Brain shrinkage observed in HSV-1 infected mice

entities.name: memory deficits
entities.description: Memory impairment observed in HSV-1 infected mice

entities.name: complement activation
entities.description: Complement cascade pathway involved in immune-mediated neuronal destruction in AD

entities.name: endocytosis
entities.description: Cellular internalization pathway used by HSV-1 for entry

entities.name: ubiquitin-proteasome system
entities.description: Protein degradation pathway whose components bind HSV-1

entities.name: apoptosis
entities.description: Programmed cell death pathway; components bound by HSV-1

entities.name: complement-mediated neuronal lysis
entities.description: Proposed mechanism of neuronal destruction in AD via complement membrane attack complex

entities.name: immune-mediated neuroinflammation
entities.description: Inflammation-mediated neuronal destruction resulting from immune defence against HSV-1

entities.name: cortex
entities.description: Cerebral cortex, site of neuronal loss in HSV-1 mouse model

entities.name: hippocampus
entities.description: Hippocampal region, site of neuronal loss in HSV-1 mouse model
